zh: Veronica persica Poir. (阿拉伯婆婆纳)
py: ā lā bó pó pó nà
desc: An herb from the Scrophulariaceae family traditionally used in Traditional Chinese Medicine for its heat-clearing and toxicity-resolving properties. Contains bioactive compounds supporting natural healing mechanisms. Research continues to validate its role in addressing heat-clearing and toxicity-resolving conditions.
uses: Enters the Lung meridian with its inherent heat-clearing and cooling properties. Traditionally used to address conditions reflecting the herb's energetic nature. Effective when properly selected and combined with complementary herbs.

desc: A perennial herb belonging to the Primulaceae family, rich in flavonoid compounds. Long used in Traditional Chinese Medicine for its heat-clearing properties and its ability to resolve inflammatory conditions. The plant contains bioactive glycosides contributing to its therapeutic potential.
uses: Clears heat and resolves toxicity with particular effectiveness for inflammatory bowel conditions. Traditionally applied for acute infections, skin eruptions, and hemorrhagic conditions. The herb's cooling property makes it valuable for heat-type infections.

desc: A woody shrub from the Alangiaceae family featuring alkaloid-rich roots and bark. Extensively documented in Traditional Chinese Medicine for its pain-relieving and circulation-promoting actions. The plant's complex alkaloid profile supports its use in addressing blood stasis conditions.
uses: Invigorates blood and relieves pain, especially for joint and musculoskeletal conditions. Effectively addresses stagnant blood patterns manifesting as arthritis and traumatic injury. Warming meridian pathways to ease movement and restore circulation.

desc: A climbing vine in the Liliaceae family with rhizomes containing steroidal saponins and flavonoids. A cornerstone herb in Traditional Chinese Medicine for clearing damp-heat and resolving toxicity. The saponin constituents provide immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects.
uses: Clears damp-heat and resolves toxicity with strong action on the Spleen meridian. Traditionally used for digestive complaints accompanying damp-heat patterns including eczema and candida infections. Also employed as a nutritive tonic for general weakness.

desc: A potent herb from the Solanaceae family containing tropane alkaloids requiring professional preparation. Valued in Traditional Chinese Medicine for rapid pain relief and respiratory support. Must be used with expert guidance due to its powerful alkaloid content.
uses: Disperses wind and rapidly relieves pain through its potent alkaloid action. Applied in specialized formulas for acute asthma, severe cough, and musculoskeletal pain. Requires professional guidance due to its powerful nature.

zh: Bletilla striata (Thunb. ex A. Murray) Rchb. f. (白及)
sci: Bletilla striata (Thunb. ex A. Murray) Rchb. f.
desc: A ground orchid from the Orchidaceae family with tuberous roots containing polysaccharides. Historically important in Traditional Chinese Medicine for hemostasis and wound healing. The plant's mucilaginous compounds promote tissue regeneration and repair.
uses: Stops bleeding and promotes wound healing with rapid hemostatic action. Traditionally used for hemoptysis, bleeding ulcers, and post-traumatic bleeding. The polysaccharides promote tissue regeneration and skin healing.

desc: A tree in the Oleaceae family with bark and leaves containing iridoid glycosides. Applied in Traditional Chinese Medicine for invigorating blood circulation and relieving pain. The plant demonstrates anti-inflammatory and circulatory-enhancing properties.
uses: Invigorates blood and moves qi to relieve pain and stiffness in joints and muscles. Effectively addresses painful obstruction patterns from cold stagnation or old trauma. Warms the meridian pathways to restore free flow.

desc: A woody vine from the Vitaceae family rich in resveratrol and polyphenolic compounds. Employed in Traditional Chinese Medicine for moving qi and supporting liver function. The plant's antioxidant profile addresses both acute inflammation and chronic degenerative conditions.
uses: Supports liver function and promotes healthy qi circulation. Applied for stress-related tension and emotional constraint affecting digestion. The plant's polyphenols provide antioxidant protection.

desc: An herbaceous plant in the Papaveraceae family containing alkaloids and mustard oil glycosides. Used in Traditional Chinese Medicine for warming the interior and promoting circulation to areas of obstruction. The plant's pungent constituents facilitate qi movement.
uses: Warms the interior and promotes circulation to areas of cold obstruction. Effectively addresses cold-type pain conditions in the abdomen and joints. The pungent nature facilitates qi movement and pain relief.

desc: A climbing herb in the Asclepiadaceae family with tuberous roots containing cardiac glycosides and alkaloids. Traditional Chinese Medicine uses this herb for clearing heat and invigorating blood circulation. The root contains compounds with documented effects on circulation and inflammation.
uses: Clears heat and invigorates blood circulation with focus on the Lung meridian. Applied for chronic cough from heat in the lungs and circulatory sluggishness. The root's compounds address both acute and chronic heat patterns.

desc: A perennial plant in the Ranunculaceae family with rhizomes rich in protoanemonin precursors. Employed in Traditional Chinese Medicine for its wind-dispersing and pain-relieving properties. The plant's volatile constituents ease muscular tension and joint stiffness.
uses: Disperses wind and relieves pain particularly for nerve pain and muscle tension. Effectively addresses wind-cold patterns causing pain and stiffness. The pungent nature helps relax muscles and improve circulation.
